mainmatter.p: Let \(E\) be displayed over \(B\). Prove that the total category\(\widetilde{E^{\tilde{\mathsf{o}}}}\) is \({\left(\widetilde{E}\right)}^{\mathsf{o}}\), and its projection functor is \({\left(p_{E}\right)}^{\mathsf{o}} : \widetilde{E}^{\mathsf{o}}\toB^{\mathsf{o}}\).

frontmatter.title: The Total Opposite of a Displayed Category

mainmatter.p: Let \(E\) be displayed over \(B\); we define its total opposite \(E^{\tilde{\mathsf{o}}}\) displayed over \(B^{\mathsf{o}}\) as follows:

ol.li: An object of \(E^{\tilde{\mathsf{o}}}_{x}\) is given by an object of \(E_{x}\).

Given \(f : x \to y\in B^{\mathsf{o}}\), a displayed morphism \(\bar{x}\xrightarrow[f]{} \bar{y}\) in \(E^{\tilde{\mathsf{o}}}\) is given by a displayed morphism \(\bar{y}\xrightarrow[f]{} \bar{x}\) in \(E\).

mainmatter.p: Warning. Do not confuse this construction with the opposite fibered category, which produces a displayed category over \(B\) and not \(B^{\mathsf{o}}\).

Let \(E\) be displayed over \(B\). Prove that the total category\(\widetilde{E^{\tilde{\mathsf{o}}}}\) is \({\left(\widetilde{E}\right)}^{\mathsf{o}}\), and its projection functor is \({\left(p_{E}\right)}^{\mathsf{o}} : \widetilde{E}^{\mathsf{o}}\toB^{\mathsf{o}}\).

Let \(E\) be displayed over \(B\), and let \(f:x\to y\in B\). Prove that a morphism \(\bar{f}:\bar{x}\xrightarrow[f]{}\bar{y}\) is cartesian over \(f\) in \(E\) if and only if \(\bar{f}:\bar{y}\xrightarrow[f]{}\bar{x}\) is cocartesian over \(f\) in \(E^{\tilde{\mathsf{o}}}\).
Prove that a displayed category \(E\) is a cartesian fibration over \(B\) if and only if \(E^{\tilde{\mathsf{o}}}\) is a cocartesian fibration over \(B^{\mathsf{o}}\).

frontmatter.title: The Total Category and its Projection

mainmatter.p: Note that any displayed category \(E\) over \(B\) can be viewed as an undisplayed category \(\widetilde{E}\) equipped with a projection functor \(p_{E}: \widetilde{E}\to B\); in this case \(\widetilde{E}\) is called the total category of \(E\).

ol.li: An object of \(\widetilde{E}\) is given by a pair \((x,\bar{x})\) where \(x\in B\) and \(\bar{x}\in E_{x}\).

A morphism \({(x,\bar{x})}\xrightarrow{}{(y,\bar{y})}\) in \(\widetilde{E}\) is given by a pair \((f,\bar{f})\) where \({x}\xrightarrow{f}{y}\) and \({\bar{x}}\xrightarrow[f]{\bar{f}}{\bar{y}}:\bar{x}\).

mainmatter.p: The construction of the total category of a displayed category is called the Grothendieck construction.
Prove that the total category \(\widetilde{\underline{B}}\) of the fundamental self-indexing is the arrow category \(B^{\to}\), and the projection is the codomain functor.
